Rather than responding to isolated prompts, it can break down complex tasks, coordinate tool use, and produce structured outputs like tables, reports, plans, and code, across multi-step workflows.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eWhat makes Kimi K2.5 especially interesting is Agent Swarm. A self-directed mode where the model can dynamically spin up and orchestrate multiple sub-agents in parallel to speed up research, verification, and execution.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIn this tutorial, I\u0026rsquo;ll cover what Kimi K2.5 is and where it performs well, then focus on four hands-on experiments that show how Agent Swarm behaves in practice, including what it does impressively, where it falls short, and when it actually beats a single-agent setup.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2 dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003eWhat Is Kimi K2.5?\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eKimi K2.5 is Moonshot AI\u0026rsquo;s open-source, natively multimodal model, which is built to handle text, images, video, and documents in one system. It extends \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: none;\" href=\"https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/kimi-k2\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: underline; -webkit-text-decoration-skip: none; text-decoration-skip-ink: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eKimi K2\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e with continued pretraining on roughly 15T mixed text and visual tokens, and it\u0026rsquo;s designed to operate not just as a chatbot, but as an agentic system that can plan, use tools, and (in swarm mode) execute tasks in parallel.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIn practice, Kimi K2.5 stands out for three things:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul style=\"padding-inline-start: 48px;\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eMultimodal reasoning:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e It can read dense visuals, reason over screenshots, and follow video context to produce structured outputs.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eStrong coding performance\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e: Kimi K2.5 can turn a visual reference into working code and iterate via visual debugging.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eAgent-first execution:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e We can run this as a single agent, a tool-augmented agent, or in Agent Swarm (beta), where it can dynamically spawn many sub-agents for wide tasks like research, extraction, comparisons, and long workflows.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cimg style=\"display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;\" src=\"https://media.datacamp.com/cms/7dca7b4d783aa85d2a67913b441c44d5.png\" alt=\"Kimi K2.5 performance\" width=\"624\" height=\"391\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: center;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eSource: \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: none;\" href=\"https://www.kimi.com/blog/kimi-k2-5.html\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: underline; -webkit-text-decoration-skip: none; text-decoration-skip-ink: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eKimi K2.5\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eThe above plots summarize Kimi K2.5\u0026rsquo;s performance across four categories: Agents, Coding, Image, and Video, with Kimi shown in blue and other leading frontier models shown as gray bars.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul style=\"padding-inline-start: 48px;\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eAgents:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e Kimi is particularly strong on agent-style benchmarks, leading on HLE-Full (50.2) and BrowseComp (74.9), and performing competitively on DeepSearchQA (77.1). This supports the agentic positioning.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eCoding:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e It is competitive on SWE-Bench Verified and Multilingual benchmarks, with top closed models and clearly strong among open options, especially for practical engineering tasks.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eImage:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e Kimi also performs well on multimodal reasoning and document-heavy understanding, with solid MMMU/MathVision scores.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eVideo:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e This model shows strong performance on LongVideoBench bechmark, which is a good signal for long-horizon video understanding rather than single-frame VQA.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eYou can try Kimi K2.5 in multiple ways:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul style=\"padding-inline-start: 48px;\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eWeb-based \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: none;\" href=\"https://www.kimi.com/\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: underline; -webkit-text-decoration-skip: none; text-decoration-skip-ink: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003echat\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eAPI: \u003ccode\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: none;\" href=\"https://platform.moonshot.ai\"\u003ehttps://platform.moonshot.ai\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eOpen-source weights: \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: none;\" href=\"https://huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5/tree/main\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: underline; -webkit-text-decoration-skip: none; text-decoration-skip-ink: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eHugging Face\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: none;\" href=\"https://www.kimi.com/code/en?track_id=5928d89a-6b60-453c-a4a6-161a0da3d1c8\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: underline; -webkit-text-decoration-skip: none; text-decoration-skip-ink: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eKimi Code\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e: Via terminal and IDE integration for agentic coding\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eFor reproducible benchmarks, Moonshot recommends using the official API or verified providers via Kimi Vendor Verifier.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2 dir=\"ltr\"\u003eWhat is Agent Swarm in Kimi K2.5?\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eMost multi-agent setups today are still hand-built; you define roles, wire a workflow, and hope the orchestration holds up as tasks get bigger. Kimi K2.5 Agent Swarm flips that model.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eInstead of predefining agents and pipelines, K2.5 can self-direct a swarm, deciding when to parallelize, how many agents to spawn, what tools to use, and how to merge results, based on the task itself.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2 dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cimg style=\"display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;\" src=\"https://media.datacamp.com/cms/93e5b22cab37f65db340fa48d3892b4c.png\" alt=\"Kimi Chat\" width=\"624\" height=\"471\" /\u003e\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIt can autonomously create and coordinate an agent swarm of up to 100 sub-agents, executing parallel workflows across up to 1,500 tool calls, with no predefined roles.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch3 dir=\"ltr\"\u003eHow does Kimi K2.5 Agent Swarm work?\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eUnder the hood, Agent Swarm introduces a trainable orchestrator that learns to decompose work into parallel subtasks and schedule them efficiently:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul style=\"padding-inline-start: 48px;\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eOrchestrator decomposes the task into parallelizable chunks, such as finding sources, extracting data, verifying claims, and formatting output.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIt then instantiates sub-agents, which are typically frozen workers spun up to execute specific subtasks on demand.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eSub-agents run concurrently, each using tools such as search, browsing, code interpreter, and file creation independently.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIt finally aggregates and reconciles outputs into a final structured deliverable like a report, spreadsheet, doc, or codebase plan.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003ch3 dir=\"ltr\"\u003eWhy PARL(Parallel-Agent Reinforcement Learning) matters\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eKimi K2.5\u0026rsquo;s swarm behavior is trained using Parallel-Agent Reinforcement Learning (\u003c/span\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: none;\" href=\"https://arxiv.org/abs/2106.07551#:~:text=Despite%20remarkable%20prior%20arts%20of,available%20at%20this%20https%20URL.\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: underline; -webkit-text-decoration-skip: none; text-decoration-skip-ink: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003ePARL\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e), a training setup that makes parallelism itself a learnable skill. This matters because naive multi-agent systems often fail in two ways:\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul style=\"padding-inline-start: 48px;\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eSerial collapse:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e Even with many agents available, the system defaults to a slow single-threaded pattern.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eFake parallelism:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e It spawns agents, but the work isn\u0026rsquo;t actually parallel or doesn\u0026rsquo;t reduce latency.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003ePARL addresses this by shaping rewards over training. It encourages parallelism early and gradually shifts optimization toward end-to-end task quality, preventing fake parallelism. To make the optimization latency-aware, K2.5 evaluates performance using Critical Steps because spawning more agents only helps if it actually shortens the slowest path of execution rather than inflating coordination overhead.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eWhen the task is wide and tool-heavy, Agent Swarm can materially reduce time-to-output. Moonshot reports that, compared to a single-agent setup, K2.5 Swarm can cut execution time by ~3x to 4.5x, and internal evaluations show up to ~80% reduction in end-to-end runtime on complex workloads through true parallelization.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch2 dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003eKimi K2.5 Examples and Observations\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIn this section, I\u0026rsquo;ll share my firsthand experience testing Kimi K2.5 Agent Swarm across diverse scenarios. Each example highlights how the swarm decomposes tasks, allocates agents, and where this approach genuinely helps or falls short in practice.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch3 dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003eResearch swarm\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eFor my first experiment, I tested whether Kimi K2.5 Agent Swarm could tackle a high-stakes, real-world task like drafting a deployment plan for open-source LLMs.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003ePrompt:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cpre class=\"language-markdown\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eI want you to research best practices for deploying open-source LLMs in production. Focus on real deployment patterns rather than theory. If possible, have different agents look into, Inference stacks like vLLM, TGI, llama.cpp, Quantization strategies and hardware trade-offs, and cost control techniques. I want a short, structured guide with common architectures, trade-offs, and concrete recommendations for small teams vs large-scale deployments.\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cimg style=\"display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;\" src=\"https://media.datacamp.com/cms/7da17084a896ec7ea47ac7cfd086f438.png\" alt=\"Research swarm\" width=\"624\" height=\"359\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIn this demo, Kimi K2.5 Agent Swarm immediately decomposed the prompt into parallel research tracks and spun up three dedicated sub-agents, namely \u003ccode\u003eInferenceStackResearcher\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eQuantizationHardwareResearcher\u003c/code\u003e, and \u003ccode\u003eCostControlResearcher\u003c/code\u003e. While each subagent name refers to the work they were allocated, it then fanned out work across multiple worker personas.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eWhat I liked most was how the final output was a clean comparison table for inference stacks, along with concise recommendations and notes for quantization. You can also use the task progress bar to see how it decomposed the work upfront and tracked each subtask to completion.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":1719,"__component":"page.video-embed","videoEmbedUrl":"https://fast.wistia.net/embed/iframe/bz0arnicrv","videoMaxWidth":null,"videoCaption":null},{"id":24740,"__component":"page-content.rich-text","text":"\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eHowever, the downside is that some claims in the final write-up feel under-cited or too absolute, and the guide would be stronger with links, sources, and clearer assumptions (hardware, model size, batch size, context length), since production advice is highly context-dependent. Overall, it handled a complex, multi-part prompt and returned a crisp, immediately usable playbook that looks production-ready.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":1722,"__component":"page.video-embed","videoEmbedUrl":"https://fast.wistia.net/embed/iframe/avieecij7f","videoMaxWidth":null,"videoCaption":null},{"id":24741,"__component":"page-content.rich-text","text":"\u003ch3 dir=\"ltr\"\u003eDataset creation and annotation\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eNext, I tested Agent Swarm on a dataset-building task by asking Kimi K2.5 to generate a 100-item math word-problem benchmark with solutions and difficulty labels. I also explicitly requested it use \u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e20 agents\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e to see how closely it follows the swarm-size constraint while maintaining quality.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ch3 dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cimg style=\"display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;\" src=\"https://media.datacamp.com/cms/24999089c365e82a5b45e36a98217f3c.png\" alt=\"Dataset creation and annotation\" width=\"624\" height=\"355\" /\u003e\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003ePrompt:\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cpre class=\"language-markdown\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eBuild a small benchmark dataset of 100 math word problems with step-by-step solutions and difficulty labels. Use at least 20 agents to generate problems in parallel, verify solutions independently, and calibrate difficulty.\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eKimi K2.5 Agent Swarm treated dataset creation as a three-phase pipeline, including generation, verification, and calibration. Although the prompt asked for at least 20 agents, the system autonomously spun up 25 sub-agents, prioritizing correctness over strict adherence to the requested count. It was interesting to observe that about five agents worked actively at a time while others queued and resumed as earlier subtasks completed, which suggests an internal scheduling mechanism.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eEach generation agent handled a distinct math domain and produced five problems with step-by-step solutions, after which 10 agents were allocated to verification, and a smaller set of five agents focused on difficulty calibration.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":1720,"__component":"page.video-embed","videoEmbedUrl":"https://fast.wistia.net/embed/iframe/9cii5ng6uq","videoMaxWidth":null,"videoCaption":null},{"id":24742,"__component":"page-content.rich-text","text":"\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eThe standout strength here is the clear separation of concerns and redundancy in verification, which materially improves dataset quality. The main downside is latency and a sequential bottleneck.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eThe full run took roughly 20\u0026ndash;25 minutes, making this approach better suited for high-quality benchmark creation than rapid iteration. Even with 25 agents, some agents had to wait for others to finish, meaning the swarm is still limited by the logic of the steps.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eStill, the ability to dynamically adjust agent count for each subtask, reuse idle agents, and converge on a labeled dataset is a strong demonstration of how Agent Swarm behaves.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":1721,"__component":"page.video-embed","videoEmbedUrl":"https://fast.wistia.net/embed/iframe/0128fuxlsq","videoMaxWidth":null,"videoCaption":null},{"id":24743,"__component":"page-content.rich-text","text":"\u003ch3 dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003eMultimodal QA at scale\u0026nbsp;\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eNext, I pushed Kimi K2.5 into a multimodal workflow by providing multiple nutrition-label images and asking for a structured comparison. The goal was to see how well it can extract, normalize, and verify visual data.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003ePrompt\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e:\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cpre class=\"language-markdown\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003eI\u0026rsquo;m sharing images of nutrition labels from 7 packaged food items. Analyze them and produce a clear comparison specifically:\nExtract fields like: calories, protein, total fat, sugar, sodium,  from each label.Normalize all values to a per-100g basis so items are comparableDouble-check numeric consistency (e.g. serving size vs totals)Rank the products from healthiest to least healthy, explaining the reasoningReturn a comparison table with normalized values for all items. Also include a summary highlighting, best high-protein option, the lowest sugar option, and items to avoid and why.\nAssume the labels may vary in format and serving size. Resolve ambiguities where possible and note any missing or unclear values.\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cimg style=\"display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;\" src=\"https://media.datacamp.com/cms/af71b940915fff930a16fadcc905fd1b.png\" alt=\"Multimodal QA at scale \" width=\"624\" height=\"357\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIn this multimodal QA experiment, Kimi K2.5 Agent Swarm decomposed the task around one label per sub-agent, spinning up seven parallel sub-agents to extract nutritional fields from each image while a coordinating agent handled normalization, cross-checking, and ranking.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eAn interesting thing about Kimi is that the constraints you specify in the prompt (extraction, normalization, verification, ranking) effectively become the blueprint for how Kimi instantiates and assigns sub-agents.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eWhat stood out for me is how the swarm treated verification as an important step, i.e, after extraction, the orchestrator explicitly reconciled serving sizes, normalized values, and flagged ambiguities instead of silently guessing. The final synthesis step, which ranked products and called out best high-protein and low-sugar options, demonstrated strong aggregation logic across visual inputs.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":1723,"__component":"page.video-embed","videoEmbedUrl":"https://fast.wistia.net/embed/iframe/ktdmpyiltj","videoMaxWidth":null,"videoCaption":null},{"id":24744,"__component":"page-content.rich-text","text":"\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eOn the downside, analyzing 7 high-res images with a swarm is compute-heavy, making it a premium feature rather than a quick-chat tool.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eThis example also exposed a current limitation; while image-based multimodal analysis worked well, direct video input was not supported, so a follow-up attempt to generate a recipe from a cooking video could not be executed.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eOverall, this demo highlights Agent Swarm\u0026rsquo;s strength in parallel visual extraction and structured reconciliation.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":1724,"__component":"page.video-embed","videoEmbedUrl":"https://fast.wistia.net/embed/iframe/3rt1ra2wbt","videoMaxWidth":null,"videoCaption":null},{"id":24745,"__component":"page-content.rich-text","text":"\u003ch3 dir=\"ltr\"\u003eCoding up a game\u003c/h3\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eFinally, I tested Kimi K2.5\u0026rsquo;s coding capabilities by asking it to build a fully interactive Bubble Shooter game from scratch. This experiment highlights how the model handles end-to-end, stateful code generation where coherence matters more than parallelism.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: bold; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003ePrompt:\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e```markdown\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eBuild a fully playable Bubble Shooter game that runs locally in a web browser. The game should support mouse and touch aiming, show a live aim guideline, fire colored bubbles from the bottom center, bounce shots off side walls, and snap bubbles to a grid at the top on collision.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eImplement core gameplay logic including popping groups of three or more connected bubbles of the same color, dropping any disconnected clusters, tracking and displaying score, showing a next-bubble preview, and ending the game when bubbles cross a danger line near the bottom.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eKeep visuals simple but responsive, include a restart button, and finish with brief instructions on how to run and test the game locally.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003e```\u003c/span\u003e\u003cimg style=\"display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;\" src=\"https://media.datacamp.com/cms/7f40985034f151426cf35bd1d9dea301.png\" alt=\"Coding up a game\" width=\"624\" height=\"340\" /\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIn this game-building experiment, Kimi K2.5 Agent Swarm made a notably conservative choice by allocating just a single sub-agent to handle the entire task.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eThat decision itself is an interesting observation because the system correctly recognized that this was a tightly coupled, stateful coding problem where heavy parallelism would add coordination overhead without any benefit.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eThe agent produced a complete, locally runnable Bubble Shooter implementation with aiming, wall bounces, grid snapping, match-three popping, score tracking, and restart logic, showing both gameplay mechanics and UI flow.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":1725,"__component":"page.video-embed","videoEmbedUrl":"https://fast.wistia.net/embed/iframe/pjkz1y79ra","videoMaxWidth":null,"videoCaption":null},{"id":24746,"__component":"page-content.rich-text","text":"\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eWhat I particularly liked is that the output felt coherent and integrated, but the trade-off, however, is that refinement and polish would likely require iterative prompting, since everything runs through a single agent.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eOverall, this example nicely demonstrates that Agent Swarm in Kimi K2.5 is not about always spawning more agents, but about using parallelism only when it genuinely improves outcomes.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":1726,"__component":"page.video-embed","videoEmbedUrl":"https://fast.wistia.net/embed/iframe/8h71csntll","videoMaxWidth":null,"videoCaption":null},{"id":24747,"__component":"page-content.rich-text","text":"\u003ch2 dir=\"ltr\"\u003eConclusion\u003c/h2\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIn this tutorial, Kimi K2.5 demonstrated an ability to decompose complex tasks, allocate agents dynamically, and produce structured, decision-ready outputs that would traditionally require a small team rather than a single model.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eAt the same time, the examples make its boundaries clear.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eAgent Swarm excels on wide, tool-heavy workflows like research, dataset construction, and multimodal extraction, but offers little advantage for tightly coupled, stateful tasks such as interactive game development.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIt also introduces real trade-offs around latency, cost, and iteration speed, especially when verification and reconciliation are prioritized over raw throughput.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"ltr\" style=\"text-align: justify;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eOverall, Kimi K2.5 offers a compelling blueprint for where agentic systems are headed. For developers and researchers comfortable experimenting with agent-based workflows, it\u0026rsquo;s one of the most interesting models to explore today.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong id=\"docs-internal-guid-3ce3318b-7fff-bc7b-faf9-e7b178e2f068\" style=\"font-weight: normal;\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eIf you\u0026rsquo;re keen to learn more about how agentic AI works, I recommend checking out the \u003c/span\u003e\u003ca style=\"text-decoration: none;\" href=\"https://www.datacamp.com/tracks/ai-agent-fundamentals\"\u003e\u003cspan style=\"background-color: transparent; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; text-decoration: underline; -webkit-text-decoration-skip: none; text-decoration-skip-ink: none; vertical-align: baseline; white-space: pre-wrap;\"\u003eAI Agent Fundamentals skill track\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":7274,"__component":"page-content.faq","title":"Kimi K2.5 and Agent Swarm FAQs","hideFaq":null,"theme":"SubtleGrey","entries":[{"id":38996,"question":"Is Kimi K2.5 truly open source and free to use?","answer":"\u003cp\u003eYes, Moonshot AI has released Kimi K2.5 under a \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"2\" data-index-in-node=\"99\"\u003eModified MIT License\u003c/strong\u003e with weights available on Hugging Face. However, because it is a massive \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"2\" data-index-in-node=\"193\"\u003e1.04 Trillion parameter\u003c/strong\u003e model (Mixture-of-Experts), running it locally requires enterprise-grade hardware (e.g., multiple H100 GPUs). For most users, the official API or Web UI is the most practical access point.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":38997,"question":"What is the Kimi K2.5 API pricing?","answer":"\u003cp\u003eKimi K2.5 offers competitive API pricing: \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"3\" data-index-in-node=\"87\"\u003e$0.60 per 1M input tokens\u003c/strong\u003e (dropping to \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"3\" data-index-in-node=\"126\"\u003e$0.10\u003c/strong\u003e for cached inputs) and \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"3\" data-index-in-node=\"155\"\u003e$3.00 per 1M output tokens\u003c/strong\u003e.\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":38998,"question":"What is the difference between Standard Chat and Agent Swarm?","answer":"\u003cp\u003eStandard chat is linear and single-threaded. \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"4\" data-index-in-node=\"110\"\u003eAgent Swarm\u003c/strong\u003e is a parallel orchestrator: it can autonomously break a prompt into sub-tasks and spawn up to \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"4\" data-index-in-node=\"216\"\u003e100 specialized sub-agents\u003c/strong\u003e to execute them simultaneously. Swarm is best for \"wide\" tasks like deep research or bulk data extraction, while Chat is better for stateful, sequential tasks like coding a game.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":38999,"question":"Does Kimi K2.5 support video input?","answer":"\u003cp\u003eYes, the model is natively multimodal and supports video input up to \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"5\" data-index-in-node=\"108\"\u003e2K resolution\u003c/strong\u003e. While it excels at long-context video benchmarks (like LongVideoBench), the feature is currently \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"5\" data-index-in-node=\"220\"\u003eexperimental\u003c/strong\u003e, meaning it may occasionally struggle with complex instruction following in video-based agent workflows compared to static images.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"id":39000,"question":"What is the context window size for Kimi K2.5?","answer":"\u003cp\u003eKimi K2.5 supports a \u003cstrong data-path-to-node=\"6\" data-index-in-node=\"71\"\u003e256,000 token context window\u003c/strong\u003e (approx. 200,000 words). This ultra-long context allows the model to process entire books, massive codebases, or long legal documents in a single pass without needing to chunk data, which is critical for maintaining accuracy in large swarms.\u003c/p\u003e"}]},{"id":9866,"__component":"page.author-biography","role":null,"author":{"id":2516,"fullName":"Aashi Dutt","biography":"\u003cp\u003eI am a Google Developers Expert in ML(Gen AI), a Kaggle 3x Expert, and a Women Techmakers Ambassador with 3+ years of experience in tech. This course breaks down the essential components of agentic workflows and explains how to build, customize, and optimize them. Youll learn the key principles behind LangChain agents, including configuring prompts, integrating tools, and managing complex workflows with LangGraph. By the end of this course, youll be equipped to build intelligent systems that automate complex tasks, enhance productivity, and provide dynamic solutions tailored to specific business needs.\u003c/p\u003e","algoliaItem":"37994__Designing Agentic Systems with LangChain__app.datacamp.com/learn/courses/designing-agentic-systems-with-langchain__course"},{"id":36538,"description":"\u003cp\u003eAI agents are changing how we work with data and software. From automating workflows to helping users navigate complex tasks, agents can search, reason, and act on your behalf. In this course, you\u0026rsquo;ll learn how to build agents using smolagents, a lightweight Python framework developed by Hugging Face. Get Hands-On With Code Agents and Tools You\u0026rsquo;ll start by understanding what makes code agents different and why theyre so powerful. Then, you\u0026rsquo;ll build your first agent from scratch, using smolagents to generate and execute Python code. You\u0026rsquo;ll also learn how to plug in built-in tools and create custom tools to extend what your agents can do. Make Agents Smarter With RAG and Memory Next, you\u0026rsquo;ll use retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to help agents pull info from large document collections. You\u0026rsquo;ll take things further by building agentic RAG systems\u0026mdash;agents that reason over multiple steps to get better answers. You\u0026rsquo;ll also learn how to add memory so agents can handle follow-up questions naturally and keep track of what\u0026rsquo;s already been done. Coordinate Multi-Agent Systems and Validate Outputs In the final chapter, you\u0026rsquo;ll build multi-agent systems that coordinate specialist agents through a manager. You\u0026rsquo;ll add planning intervals, use callbacks for insight into agent behavior, and validate final answers, so your agents stay reliable and user-friendly. By the end of the course, you\u0026rsquo;ll know how to build agents that think ahead, work together, and get things done.\u003c/p\u003e","algoliaItem":"43797__AI Agents with Hugging Face smolagents__app.datacamp.com/learn/courses/ai-agents-with-hugging-face-smolagents__course"},{"id":36539,"description":"\u003cp\u003eBuild a customer-support assistant step-by-step with Google\u0026rsquo;s Agent Development Kit: start from a one-line greeting bot, then add tools for FAQ look-ups, policy guardrails for safety, and multi-agent delegation for complex queries.       })(window,document,'profitwell','script','https://public.profitwell.com/js/profitwell.js');</script><script id="breadcrumb_schema" type="application/ld+json" data-nscript="afterInteractive">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"BreadcrumbList","itemListElement":[{"@type":"ListItem","item":"https://www.datacamp.com","name":"Home","position":1},{"@type":"ListItem","item":"https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial","name":"Tutorials","position":2},{"@type":"ListItem","item":"https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/category/ai","name":"Artificial Intelligence","position":3},{"@type":"ListItem","name":"Kimi K2.5 and Agent Swarm: A Guide With Four Practical Examples","position":4}]}</script><script id="faq_schema" type="application/ld+json" data-nscript="afterInteractive">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"<p>Yes, Moonshot AI has released Kimi K2.5 under a <strong data-path-to-node=\"2\" data-index-in-node=\"99\">Modified MIT License</strong> with weights available on Hugging Face. However, because it is a massive <strong data-path-to-node=\"2\" data-index-in-node=\"193\">1.04 Trillion parameter</strong> model (Mixture-of-Experts), running it locally requires enterprise-grade hardware (e.g., multiple H100 GPUs). For most users, the official API or Web UI is the most practical access point.</p>"},"name":"Is Kimi K2.5 truly open source and free to use?"},{"@type":"Question","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"<p>Kimi K2.5 offers competitive API pricing: <strong data-path-to-node=\"3\" data-index-in-node=\"87\">$0.60 per 1M input tokens</strong> (dropping to <strong data-path-to-node=\"3\" data-index-in-node=\"126\">$0.10</strong> for cached inputs) and <strong data-path-to-node=\"3\" data-index-in-node=\"155\">$3.00 per 1M output tokens</strong>. </p>"},"name":"What is the Kimi K2.5 API pricing?"},{"@type":"Question","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"<p>Standard chat is linear and single-threaded. <strong data-path-to-node=\"4\" data-index-in-node=\"110\">Agent Swarm</strong> is a parallel orchestrator: it can autonomously break a prompt into sub-tasks and spawn up to <strong data-path-to-node=\"4\" data-index-in-node=\"216\">100 specialized sub-agents</strong> to execute them simultaneously. Swarm is best for \"wide\" tasks like deep research or bulk data extraction, while Chat is better for stateful, sequential tasks like coding a game.</p>"},"name":"What is the difference between Standard Chat and Agent Swarm?"},{"@type":"Question","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"<p>Yes, the model is natively multimodal and supports video input up to <strong data-path-to-node=\"5\" data-index-in-node=\"108\">2K resolution</strong>. While it excels at long-context video benchmarks (like LongVideoBench), the feature is currently <strong data-path-to-node=\"5\" data-index-in-node=\"220\">experimental</strong>, meaning it may occasionally struggle with complex instruction following in video-based agent workflows compared to static images.</p>"},"name":"Does Kimi K2.5 support video input?"},{"@type":"Question","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"<p>Kimi K2.5 supports a <strong data-path-to-node=\"6\" data-index-in-node=\"71\">256,000 token context window</strong> (approx. 200,000 words). This ultra-long context allows the model to process entire books, massive codebases, or long legal documents in a single pass without needing to chunk data, which is critical for maintaining accuracy in large swarms.</p>"},"name":"What is the context window size for Kimi K2.5?"}]}</script><next-route-announcer><p aria-live="assertive" id="__next-route-announcer__" role="alert" style="border: 0px; clip: rect(0px, 0px, 0px, 0px); height: 1px; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; padding: 0px; position: absolute; top: 0px; width: 1px; white-space: nowrap; overflow-wrap: normal;"></p></next-route-announcer><script async="" id="dcTrackingGenerator" src="https://compliance.datacamp.com/base.js"></script><div id="usercentrics-root" data-created-at="1784342641427" style=""></div><script src="https://campaign-service.datacamp.com/api/v1/banner?locale=en" referrerpolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" data-nscript="lazyOnload"></script><script src="https://cdn.onesignal.com/sdks/OneSignalSDK.js" data-nscript="lazyOnload"></script><script id="profitwell-start" data-nscript="lazyOnload">profitwell('start', {});</script><script src="https://cdn.requestmetrics.com/agent/current/rm.js" async="" data-nscript="lazyOnload"></script><div style="display: none; visibility: hidden;"><script type="text/javascript" nonce="null">(function(){function f(){window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("dc:heap:loaded"))}function g(a){function b(c){var d=RegExp("^/portfolio/([^/]+)+(.*)*","i"),e=c.match(d);e&&e[1]&&e[1]!=="discovery"&&(c=c.replace(d,function(k,l,h){return"/portfolio/****"+(h||"")}));return c}a.pageview_properties&&(a.pageview_properties.path&&(a.pageview_properties.path=b(a.pageview_properties.path)),a.pageview_properties.previous_page&&(a.pageview_properties.previous_page=b(a.pageview_properties.previous_page)));a.session_properties&&
